items.title: Machine Learning
items.description: ML is the dominant modern subset of AI: systems that improve on a task by learning patterns from data. The sibling topic owns the canonical ML definition.

items.title: Supervised learning
items.description: Supervised learning is a concrete ML paradigm—labeled examples guide training—showing how 'learning from data' instantiates one branch of AI in practice.

items.title: What is Generative AI?
items.description: Generative AI is a further specialization under deep learning. Product-level Gen AI definitions, model types, and applications live in genai-fundamentals—not duplicated here.

items.title: Responsible & Ethical GenAI
items.description: Once AI systems affect people at scale, principles, risks, and governance matter. This topic frames scope; responsible deployment is developed in genai-fundamentals.

items.title: Deep Learning
items.description: Deep learning uses multi-layer neural networks as the learning mechanism inside ML—covered in the sibling topic without repeating here.

items.title: Generative AI (sibling)
items.description: Generative AI systems create new content (text, images, code). High-level placement under AI is here; technical and product depth is in the sibling and genai-fundamentals topics.
